017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.util.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.adapter.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.http.header.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
031
032/**
033 * Response processor that serializes
034 * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807">RFC 7807 / 9457</a> {@link Problem} payloads as
035 * {@code application/problem+json}.
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * Fires when the response content is one of the following (in order):
039 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
040 *    <li>A {@link Problem} bean &mdash; serialized directly. Honors the client {@code Accept} header.
041 *    <li>A {@link ProblemException} &mdash; unwrapped via {@link ProblemException#getProblem()}. Honors the
042 *       client {@code Accept} header.
043 *    <li>A {@link BasicHttpException} &mdash; adapted via a registered {@link ProblemMapper} (if any matches),
044 *       else via {@link ProblemAdapters#fromException(BasicHttpException)}. This branch handles the common throw
045 *       path where the operation handler throws a {@code BasicHttpException} and the framework sets it as the
046 *       response content. <b>Gated by {@code isProblemDetails()}</b> on the {@link RestOpContext} (per-op
047 *       {@code @RestGet(problemDetails)} / {@code @RestPost(problemDetails)} / etc., falling back to the
048 *       resource-level {@code @Rest(problemDetails)} opt-in via tri-state inheritance). When opted-in, the
049 *       {@code Accept} header is ignored on this branch (errors always emit {@code application/problem+json}; see
050 *       RFC 7807).
051 *    <li>Any other {@link Throwable} &mdash; only when an explicit {@link ProblemMapper} matches its class
052 *       hierarchy. Gated by the same opt-in. Mappers that return {@code null} are skipped and the chain
053 *       continues; if no mapper matches, the processor returns {@code NEXT}.
054 * </ul>
055 *
056 * <h5 class='section'>Mapper discovery</h5>
057 * <p>
058 * The processor pulls registered {@link ProblemMapper}s from the resource's bean store in this order:
059 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
060 *    <li>A {@link ProblemMapperList} bean &mdash; treated as the authoritative ordered registry. Recommended
061 *       when more than one mapper is registered, since the underlying {@code @Bean} bean-store walk pairs each
062 *       factory method with its return type and so collapses multiple
063 *       {@code @Bean public ProblemMapper foo()} entries on the same resource into a single slot.
064 *    <li>A single {@link ProblemMapper} bean (via {@code BeanStore.getBean(ProblemMapper.class)}) &mdash; the
065 *       conventional single-mapper registration pattern.
066 * </ul>
067 * <p>
068 * For a given thrown exception the processor walks the collected mappers and picks the one whose
069 * {@link ProblemMapper#getExceptionType()} sits closest to the thrown class in the hierarchy (most-specific
070 * wins; ties broken by registration order). The chosen mapper's {@link ProblemMapper#map(Throwable)} return
071 * value replaces the default adaptation; a {@code null} return falls through to the next-most-specific mapper,
072 * and ultimately to the built-in {@link ProblemAdapters#fromException(BasicHttpException)} fallback for
073 * {@link BasicHttpException} subclasses.
074 *
075 * <h5 class='section'>Localization seam</h5>
076 * <p>
077 * Before serializing, the processor consults the bean store for a {@link ProblemLocalizationStrategy} bean and
078 * passes the resolved {@link Problem} (and {@code RestRequest.getLocale()}) through it. The default behavior is
079 * {@link ProblemLocalizationStrategy#IDENTITY} (pass-through) &mdash; this is a future-work seam intentionally
080 * shipped without a built-in {@code Messages}/resource-bundle implementation. A {@code null} strategy return is
081 * treated as &quot;no opinion&quot; and the pre-localization {@link Problem} is used instead.
082 *
083 * <p>
084 * <b>Status code policy.</b> When {@link Problem#getStatus()} is non-{@code null}, the processor calls
085 * {@code RestResponse.setStatus(int)} with that value. Otherwise it leaves the existing status alone &mdash;
086 * {@code RestSession.run()} normalizes {@code 0} to {@code 200} so per-op default codes (e.g. {@code @RestPost}
087 * default of 200) still flow through.
088 *
089 * <p>
090 * <b>{@code type} field policy.</b> Serialized as-is. A {@code null} {@code type} is omitted from the JSON output
091 * rather than synthesized as {@code "type":"about:blank"} on the wire (preserves the absent-vs-explicit distinction).
092 *
093 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
094 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Problem}
095 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ProblemException}
096 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ProblemMapper}
097 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ProblemLocalizationStrategy}
098 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ProblemAdapters}
099 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ResponseProcessors">Response Processors</a>
100 * </ul>
101 */
102public class ProblemDetailsProcessor implements ResponseProcessor {
103
104   private static final String PROBLEM_JSON = ContentType.APPLICATION_PROBLEM_JSON.getValue();
105
106   @Override /* Overridden from ResponseProcessor */
107   @SuppressWarnings({
108      "resource",  // negotiated output stream owned by the response; closed by the container/RestCall
109      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for RFC-9457 problem-details response dispatch
110   })
111   public int process(RestOpSession opSession) throws IOException {
112
113      var res = opSession.getResponse();
114      var raw = res.getContent(Object.class);
115
116      if (raw == null)
117         return NEXT;
118
119      Problem problem;
120      boolean isErrorPath;
121
122      if (raw instanceof Problem p) {
123         problem = p;
124         isErrorPath = false;
125      } else if (raw instanceof ProblemException pe) {
126         problem = pe.getProblem();
127         isErrorPath = opSession.getContext().isProblemDetails();
128      } else if (raw instanceof BasicHttpException e) {
129         if (! opSession.getContext().isProblemDetails())
130            return NEXT;
131         problem = mapException(opSession, e);
132         isErrorPath = true;
133      } else if (raw instanceof Throwable t) {
134         if (! opSession.getContext().isProblemDetails())
135            return NEXT;
136         problem = mapException(opSession, t);
137         if (problem == null)
138            return NEXT;
139         isErrorPath = true;
140      } else {
141         return NEXT;
142      }
143
144      if (problem == null)
145         return NEXT;
146
147      // Q5: error path ignores Accept (RFC 7807 §3); success path honors Accept strictly.
148      if (! isErrorPath) {
149         var accept = opSession.getRequest().getHeaderParam("Accept").orElse("");
150         if (! acceptsProblemJson(accept))
151            return NEXT;
152      }
153
154      problem = localize(opSession, problem);
155
156      var status = problem.getStatus();
157      if (status != null)
158         res.setStatus(status.intValue());
159
160      res.setHeader(ContentType.APPLICATION_PROBLEM_JSON);
161
162      try {
163         var os = res.getNegotiatedOutputStream();
164         JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(problem, os);
165         os.flush();
166         os.finish();
167      } catch (SerializeException e) {
168         throw new IOException("Failed to serialize Problem payload", e);
169      }
170
171      return FINISHED;
172   }
173
174   /**
175    * Resolves a {@link Problem} from a thrown exception by consulting registered {@link ProblemMapper}s in
176    * most-specific-first order, falling back to {@link ProblemAdapters#fromException(BasicHttpException)} for
177    * {@link BasicHttpException} subclasses when no mapper matches (or all matching mappers return {@code null}).
178    *
179    * @return The {@link Problem} to emit, or {@code null} when no mapper opined and the exception is not a
180    *    {@link BasicHttpException}.
181    */
182   @SuppressWarnings({
183      "unchecked", // ProblemMapper#map is parameterized over the matched throwable subtype.
184      "rawtypes"   // ProblemMapper#map is parameterized over the matched throwable subtype.
185   })
186   private static Problem mapException(RestOpSession opSession, Throwable thrown) {
187      for (var mapper : resolveMappers(opSession, thrown.getClass())) {
188         var result = ((ProblemMapper) mapper).map(thrown);
189         if (result != null)
190            return result;
191      }
192      if (thrown instanceof BasicHttpException e)
193         return ProblemAdapters.fromException(e);
194      return null;
195   }
196
197   /**
198    * Returns all registered {@link ProblemMapper}s whose {@link ProblemMapper#getExceptionType()} is assignable
199    * from {@code thrownClass}, sorted most-specific-first. A {@link ProblemMapperList} bean (if present) is
200    * consulted first; a single {@link ProblemMapper} bean (if no list is registered) is consulted as a
201    * fallback. Registration order serves as the tiebreaker for mappers at the same hierarchy depth.
202    */
203   @SuppressWarnings({
204      "resource" // RestOpSession owns this bean store lifecycle.
205   })
206   private static List<ProblemMapper<?>> resolveMappers(RestOpSession opSession, Class<?> thrownClass) {
207      var bs = opSession.getBeanStore();
208      var candidates = new ArrayList<ProblemMapper<?>>();
209      bs.getBean(ProblemMapperList.class).map(ProblemMapperList::asList).ifPresent(candidates::addAll);
210      if (candidates.isEmpty())
211         bs.getBean(ProblemMapper.class).ifPresent(candidates::add);
212      if (candidates.isEmpty())
213         return List.of();
214      var matches = new ArrayList<ProblemMapper<?>>(candidates.size());
215      for (var m : candidates) {
216         if (m.getExceptionType().isAssignableFrom(thrownClass))
217            matches.add(m);
218      }
219      matches.sort((a, b) -> hierarchyDepth(a.getExceptionType(), thrownClass) - hierarchyDepth(b.getExceptionType(), thrownClass));
220      return matches;
221   }
222
223   /**
224    * Returns the number of superclass hops from {@code thrownClass} up to {@code targetType}; lower is closer.
225    * A {@code 0} return means {@code targetType} equals {@code thrownClass} (exact match); larger values mean
226    * the mapper sits higher up the class hierarchy and is therefore less specific. Sorting ascending by this
227    * value puts the most-specific mapper first.
228    */
229   private static int hierarchyDepth(Class<?> targetType, Class<?> thrownClass) {
230      var depth = 0;
231      var c = thrownClass;
232      while (c != null && c != targetType) {
233         c = c.getSuperclass();
234         depth++;
235      }
236      return c == null ? Integer.MAX_VALUE : depth;
237   }
238
239   private static Problem localize(RestOpSession opSession, Problem problem) {
240      @SuppressWarnings({
241         "resource" // RestOpSession owns this bean store lifecycle.
242      })
243      var strategy = opSession.getBeanStore().getBean(ProblemLocalizationStrategy.class).orElse(ProblemLocalizationStrategy.IDENTITY);
244      var locale = opSession.getRequest().getLocale();
245      var result = strategy.localize(problem, locale);
246      return result == null ? problem : result;
247   }
248
249   private static boolean acceptsProblemJson(String accept) {
250      if (isEmpty(accept))
251         return true;
252      return accept.contains(PROBLEM_JSON) || accept.contains("*/*");
253   }
254}
